# Asmari Formation

**Summary**: A significant carbonate reservoir and stratigraphic unit in the Middle East.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[1992-el-sahn|El-Sahn, 1992]] characterized the Asmari Formation as a 110m thick unit divided into three members.

## Depositional Environment

- [[1992-el-sahn|El-Sahn, 1992]] reef to back-reef

## Reservoir Properties



## Diagenetic History

- [[1992-el-sahn|El-Sahn, 1992]] dolomitized, recrystallized

## Key Findings Across Sources

- The Asmari Formation (fig.12) is represented by a thickness of about 110 m (samples 17-34). ([[1992-el-sahn|El-Sahn, 1992, p. 64]])
